changes.before: Sylvanas ignored him and looked at the runner.
Not a courier, then. Not anyone Cyndia had thought safe enough to define from a pouch and a seal.
changes.after: Not a courier. Cyndia had not thought her safe enough to define from a pouch and seal.

changes.before: Sylvanas used the ride the way soldiers used rides: sorting what mattered.
changes.after: Sylvanas used the ride to sort what mattered.

changes.before: A real spy did not kneel at ward-stones until the rangers found her. A real spy did not keep talking after capture unless the talking itself was the mission.
changes.after: A real spy would not have knelt at ward-stones until the rangers found her, and would not have kept talking after capture unless the talk itself was the mission.

changes.before: From inside the shed came no shouting, no rattling of chain, no offended lecture on diplomatic immunity. Just silence.
changes.after: No shouting. No chain-rattle. No lecture on diplomatic immunity. Just silence.

changes.before: Some court-bred prodigy with clean boots and a look already arranged for being underestimated. That was the type.
changes.after: Some court-bred prodigy with clean boots and a look already arranged for being underestimated.

changes.before: Her braid was trying to come loose.
changes.after: Her braid had half come loose.

changes.before: Calluses, too — not ornamental ones from training yards, but the hard skin of someone who used her staff.
changes.after: Calluses too — hard use, not training-yard polish.
The jaw stayed hard with fury.

flags: Line 59: 'civilizational dignity' kept — court-sarcasm from Lor'themar, not document voice contamination.
Line 171: 'pattern' kept in dialogue — mage vocabulary, but still plain enough for Jaina's downshifted register.
Line 195: 'leylines' kept in dialogue — load-bearing world term, not bureaucratic phrasing.

huntObservations.name: wordcount reduction
observations: Line 93 carried a redundant reaction beat before the actual question; cut the extra step.
Line 101 opened Sylvanas's assessment with a double negation scaffold; compressed to one harder judgment.
Line 141 explained how soldiers use travel time instead of letting the direct action carry it; trimmed.
Line 147 repeated 'A real spy' for the same inference twice; collapsed into one sentence.
Word count fell 2548 -> 2517 without losing a beat or changing scene order.
The pre-runner patrol stretch was re-read word by word; competence banter stayed because it establishes the field-vs-politics contrast.
The holding-room entrance was re-read word by word; only scaffolding came out, not the tension beats.
No sentence was expanded during this hunt.

huntObservations.name: hitlist patterns
observations: Line 205 had a visible negation chain; replaced with harder fragments.
Line 235 restated a judgment the previous sentence had already landed; cut the echo.
Line 239 personified the braid; reduced to a physical state.
Line 241 explained the calluses through a contrastive mini-gloss; replaced with a shorter Sylvanas-native read.
Line 255 gave fury the job of holding the face up; grounded it in the jaw.
The chapter-wide 'like' count stayed at 1, comfortably under the cap of 3.
The chapter-wide 'as if' count stayed at 0.
Filter words stayed at 2; both surviving uses carry actual sensory or auditory function rather than lazy distance.
The two 'something' hits were checked; both are load-bearing in context, so no cut was made.
No elegant variation, planning vocabulary, clinical anatomy, or document-voice narration was found in the remaining prose.

huntObservations.name: dialogue register hunt
observations: Every quoted line was scanned from line 15 through line 263.
Sylvanas's speech stayed clipped and command-heavy; no modern analyst jargon slipped in.
Lor'themar's banter stayed aristocratic and dry rather than bureaucratic.
The runner's report remained field shorthand, not memo language.
Cyndia's report used plain operational words even when describing magical anomaly.
Jaina does not speak on-page in this chapter, so no Jaina spoken-line cleanup was needed.
The world terms 'ward-stones,' 'pattern,' 'leylines,' and 'Antonidas' were checked individually and kept as canon-specific vocabulary.
Dialogue register contamination count remained 0 before and after the pass.
